Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 044 [If a person's skull suffers from fever][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* NA na-piš KIR₄-šu₂ DUGUD ṣi-⸢in⸣-[na-aḫ]-ti-ru ḫi-miṭ U₄.[DA ...]

(o 1) (If) a person's breathing is burdensome (and he has) diarrhoea: sunstroke [...]. In the morning, on an empty stomach, you wipe out his mouth with nīnû-plant. You pour alum into his nostrils. [...]. He sucks mountain honey (on) his tongue. You wash it (his mouth) with cucumber juice, hound's-tongue (and) tamarisk [...]. You crush together (and) sieve foliage of tamarisk, juniper, kukuru-tree (and) foliage of liquorice. You mix (them all) with fat. You press out (the liquid) with leather. You bandage and he will recover.

o 22

ina še-ri₃ la₃ pa-tan u₂KUR.⸢RA⸣ KA-šu₂ ta-[kap]-⸢par⸣ na₄gab-u ana na-ḫi-⸢ri-šu₂ ŠUB⸣[di ...]

o 33

EME-šu₂ LAL₃ KUR.RA u₂-na-aṭ-ṭa ina A u₂UKUŠ₂ u₂EME.⸢UR.GI₇⸣ gišŠINIG RA-su [...]

o 44

PA gišŠINIG šimLI šimGUR₂.GUR₂ PA giššu-⸢ši⸣ 1niš GAZ SIM ina I₃.UDU [ḪI].ḪI ina KUŠ SURri [(x)]; LA₂-ma ina-eš

o 55

* ⸢NA⸣ NINDA GU₇ KAŠ NAG-ma na-piš KIR₄-šu₂ DUGUD GI.GID₂.MUR-MEŠ GIG u₂KUR.RA <<IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA>>

(o 5) (If) a person eats bread, drinks beer and then his breathing is difficult: he is ill with flute of the lungs. You wipe out [...] with nīnû-plant. You pour alum into his nostrils. He sucks mountain honey (on) his tongue with pressed vine. You wash it (his mouth) [...] cucumber, tamarisk in hot water. You crush together (and) sieve juniper, kukuru-tree, ṣumlalû-plant, [...], opopanax, resin of baluhhu-plant, foliage of tamarisk, foliage of apple tree, foliage of libāru-tree. You mix [...]. You recover and dry (it). You crush (and) sieve. You mix (it) with fat. You press out (the liquid) with leather. You bandage his breast.

o 66

[...] ⸢ta⸣-kap-⸢par⸣ <IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA> ana na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ ŠUBdi giš⸢GEŠTIN⸣ SUR.RA EME-<šu₂> LAL₃ KUR.⸢RA⸣ u₂-na-aṭ-ṭa

o 77

[...] u₂UKUŠ₂ gišŠINIG ina A-MEŠ KUM₂-⸢MEŠ?⸣ RA-su šimLI šimGUR₂.GUR₂ šimGAM.MA

o 88

[...] šimḪAB ILLU ⸢šimBULUḪ⸣ PA gišŠINIG PA gišḪAŠḪUR PA gišMI.⸢PAR₃⸣ 1niš GAZ ⸢SIM⸣

o 99

[... ḪI].⸢ḪI⸣ GUR-ma ḪAD₂.DU ⸢GAZ⸣ SIM ina I₃.UDU ḪI.ḪI ina KUŠ SURri! GABA-su LA₂⸢id⸣

o 1010

[...] x x x x (x) u₂⸢ur₂⸣-ne-e I₃ gišEREN ana na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ ŠUBdi šimLI šim[GUR₂].⸢GUR₂⸣

(o 10) You pour [...] ... mint (and) cedar oil into his nostrils. You mix in water juniper, kukuru-tree, [...], opopanax, plant of [...] .... You recover and dry (it). You crush (and) sieve. You mix with fat. You press out (the liquid) with leather. You shave his head. You bandage and he will recover.

o 1111

[...] ⸢šim⸣ḪAB ⸢u₂?⸣[x] x ina A ḪI.ḪI GUR-ma ḪAD₂.A ⸢GAZ⸣ SIM ina I₃.UDU ḪI.ḪI ina KUŠ SURri!  ; SAG.DU-su SARab LA₂-ma ina-eš

o 1212

[...] DUGUD LAL₃ BABBAR u₂-na-ṣab-ma TIuṭ

(o 12) [...] is burdensome: he sucks white honey and he will live.

o 1313

[...] x ⸢ŠIM⸣ giš?ŠA₃.<<ŠA₃>>.⸢GIŠIMMAR?⸣ KA-šu₂ SA₅-ma <KUM₂?> u₂-kal u₂-tab-ba-kam-ma EGIR-šu₂ KA-šu₂ I₃.GIŠ SA₅-ma  ; u₂-tab-ba-kam-ma ina-[eš]

(o 13) You fill his mouth (with) [...] ..., aromatic substance (and) palm-milk. Then, he suffers from fever, he vomits and after it you fill his mouth (with) oil so that he vomits (again) and he will recover.

o 1414

[...] x x [... u₂?]A.ZAL.LA₂ ḪAD₂.DU SUD₂ IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA SUD₂ 1niš ḪI.ḪI x [...]

(o 14) [...] ... [...] you dry (and) pound azallû-plant. You pound alum (and then) you mix (them) together ... [...] ... he suffers from [...], after it [...] his mouth (with) oil ... [...] flows: būšānu-disease afflicts him. [...] mustard in water [...].

o 1515

[...] x [...] u₂-kal EGIR-šu₂ I₃.GIŠ KA-šu₂ x [...]

o 1616

[...] DUku bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su GAZIsar ina A-MEŠ [...]

o 1717

[... u₂]KUR.⸢RA⸣ u₂NIG₂.GIR₂.NU šimBI.KU₃.GI IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA ⸢u₂⸣[...]

(o 17) [...] nīnû-plant, ...-plant, ...-aromatic substance, alum, plant of [...], lumps of dough, the top of his mouth [...] go out. You bandage these plants [...] the top of his mouth [...].

o 1818

[...] ⸢ḫu⸣-um-bi-ṣa-a-ti [NIG₂].SILA₁₁.GA₂ UGU KA-šu₂ [...]

o 1919

[...] ⸢E₃⸣ni U₂-ḪI.A an-nu-⸢ti⸣ [...] UGU KA-šu₂ LA₂-[ma ...]

o 2020

* [... ap-pa]-šu₂ u ⸢KA⸣-šu₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-⸢nu DAB⸣it tug₂⸢GADA⸣ ta-⸢ṣap⸣-pir LAL₃ SU₃ [IM].SAḪAR.⸢NA₄⸣.[KUR.RA ...]

(o 20) (If) būšānu-disease affects [...] nose and mouth: you press down linen. You sprinkle honey. You dissolve alum, [...]. You drive (it) into his nostrils. You pound nīnû-plant (and) alum. You dissolve linen, [...]. You rub his nose and his mouth until the blood appears. ... [...] you do (it again) in the same way. [...] you crush fresh būšānu-plant. You take the juice. You mix 2 shekels of oil within it. You make him vomit on an empty stomach [...].

o 2121

⸢tu-la⸣-am ana na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ ta-[sa]-an-niš u₂KUR.RA IM.⸢SAḪAR⸣.[NA₄.KUR.RA] SUD₂ tug₂[GADA ...]

o 2222

⸢tu⸣-la-a-am KIR₄-šu₂ u KA-šu₂ ⸢EN⸣ UŠ₂ IGI ta-kar x [...] ⸢an⸣-nam DU₃⸢uš⸣ [...]

o 2323

u₂ḪAB SIG₇-su tu-pa-ṣa A-MEŠ TIqe₂ 2 GIN₂ I₃.GIŠ ana ⸢ŠA₃-šu₂-nu ḪI⸣.ḪI la₃ pa-tan tu-ša₂-aʾ-[ra-šu-ma ...]

o 2424

U₂ BABBAR ina KAŠ la₃ pa-tan NAG-šu₂ ⸢u₂?⸣x x u₂ak-tam SUD₂ ina KAŠ la₃ pa-tan [...]

(o 24) (The patient) drinks white-plant in beer on an empty stomach. You pound ...-plant (and) aktam-plant. [...] in beer on an empty stomach.

o 2525

KI.MIN BAR MUŠ tur-ar₂ SUD₂ ina I₃ u KAŠ [...]

(o 25) Ditto, you parch (and) pound a snakeskin. [...] in oil and beer [...].

o 2626

* NA ap-pa-šu₂ u ⸢KA-šu₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu⸣ DABit-ma ŠA₃-ME-šu₂ nap-ḫu ILLU LI.TAR [...]

(o 26) (If) būšānu-disease affects a person's nose and mouth and his intestines are swollen: [...] resin of abukkatu-plant [...].

o 2727

u₂⸢KUR.RA SUD₂⸣ ina I₃ u KAŠ NAG-ma ina-eš IM.KAL.⸢GUG⸣ SUD₂ ina I₃ u KAŠ ⸢NAG⸣-[ma ...]

(o 27) You pound nīnû-plant (and the patient) drinks (it) in oil and beer and he will recover. You pound kalgukku-paste (and the patient) drinks (it) [and ...].

o 2828

[IM.SAḪAR].NA₄.KUR.RA <SUD₂> ina LAL₃ I₃ u KAŠ ⸢NAG⸣-[ma ...]

(o 28) You pound alum (and the patient) drinks (it) in honey, oil and beer [...].

o 2929

* [... KIR₄?]-šu₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-ma na-ḫi-ra-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ GIG-MEŠ SA₅-MEŠ tug₂⸢GADA⸣ [...]

(o 29) (If) būšānu-disease affects a [...] nose and his nostrils hurt him (and) are full of lesions: you dissolve [...] linen [...] (and) alum. .... You blow with a reed-stem [...].

o 3030

[...] IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA tu-la-am ana x MUR-šu₂ x-ma ina giSAG.KU₅ ⸢MU₂⸣ [...]

o 3131

⸢IM.KAL.GUG⸣ SUD₂ ta-za-ru-ma ina-eš : BAR gišNU.⸢UR₂⸣.[MA] SUD₂ ta-za-ru-ma ina-eš : A x [...]; ta-za-ru-ma ina-[eš]

(o 31) You pound kalgukku-paste. You scatter (it on him) and he will recover. : You pound rind of pomegranate. You scatter (it on him) and he will recover. : [...] juice of [...]. You scatter (it on him) and he will recover.

o 3232

⸢šim⸣SES SUD₂ ta-za-ru-ma ina-eš : šum₄-ma ina na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ in-ne₂-eṣ-⸢ṣi⸣-[in ...]

(o 32) You pound myrrh. You scatter (him) and he will recover. : If (something) is smelled in his nostrils: [...] you parch (and) pound [...] of a snake. You wipe clean his nostrils. Once, twice, three times [...] to his nostrils [...].

o 3333

[...] ⸢MUŠ⸣ tur-ar₂ SUD₂ na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ tu-kap-par 1-šu₂ 2-šu₂ 3-šu₃ ana na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ [...]

o 3434

* NA na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ um-ṣa-at ḫi-li it-tab-ši na₄gab-u₂ šimSES U₂ BABBAR 1niš [...]  ; ana UGU MAR-[ma ...]

(o 34) (If) weeping mole occurs in a person's nostrils: [...] alum, myrrh, white-plant together [...]. You anoint on (his nostrils) [...].

o 3535

* NA BI.LU na-ḫi-ri-šu₂ DABtu u₂KUR.RA u₂ur₂-ne-e GAZIsar U₂ BABBAR x [...]

(o 35) (If) the būšānu-disease affects a person's nose: you pound together [...] nīnû-plant, mint, mustard, white-plant [...] alum. You press down linen. You sprinkle honey. You dissolve these plants. [...] he recovers, you repeatedly place (them).

o 3636

IM.SAḪAR.NA₄.KUR.RA 1niš SUD₂ tug₂GADA ta-ṣap-pir LAL₃ SU₃ U₂-ḪI.A an-nu-ti₃ tu-⸢lam⸣ [...]

o 3737

[...] TI.LA GAR.GARan NAGA.SI te-be₂-er u₂KUR.RA 1niš SUD₂ ina I₃.KUR.RA ḪI.ḪI [...]

(o 37) You select glasswort. You pound nīnû-plant together. You mix in naphtha. You repeatedly rub [...]. You regularly pour hot pomegranate juice into his nose. You repeatedly heat [...]. You fumigate him with nīnû-plant (and) wormwood from the fire. [...] mint in oil [...] (and) [...] into [his] nostrils.

o 3838

[ta-ak]-ta-na-ar A gišNU.UR₂.MA KUM₂-MEŠ gi-na-a ana KIR₄-šu₂ ŠUB.ŠUB [...]

o 3939

[tu-ub-ta]-na-ḫar u₂KUR.RA u₂si-i-⸢ḫu⸣ ina IZI SAR-šu₂ u₂ur₂-ne-e ina I₃ [...]; ana na-⸢ḫi⸣-[ri-šu₂ ...]

o 4040

[... bu-uʾ-ša₂]-⸢nu⸣ GIG 1 ŠE šimKU₇.KU₇ 1 ŠE šimLI 1 ⸢ŠE?⸣ x [...]

(o 40) [...] is ill with būšānu-disease: [...] 1 grain of kukuru-tree, 1 grain of juniper, 1 grain of ... [...]. You parch and pound [...], alum (and) a skin of snake [...].

o 4141

[... IM.SAḪAR].⸢NA₄⸣.KUR.RA BAR ⸢MUŠ⸣ [tur]-ar₂ SUD₂ [...]

o 4242

[...] DAB-ma la₃ DUḪ 7 ŠE ⸢UKUŠ₂⸣ [...]

(o 42) [...] affects [...] and do not release: you pound together [...] 7 grains of cucumber [...]. In oil [...].

o 4343

[...] 1niš SUD₂ ina I₃ [...]

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] ⸢BI⸣.LU ṣab-tu u₂KUR.RA u₂[...]

(r 1) [...] the būšānu-disease affects [...]: nīnû-plant, plant of [...] mint in oil ... [...].

r 22

[...] u₂ur₂-ne-e ina I₃ x x [...]

r 33

[... bu-uʾ-ša₂]-nu DABit u₂ak-tam SUD₂ ana KIR₄-[šu₂ ...]

(r 3) [...] būšānu-disease affects [...]: you pound aktam-plant. [You ...] into [his] nose [...].

r 44

[...] I UGU KIR₄-šu₂ [...]

(r 4) [...] ... the top of his nose [...].

r 55

[...] ⸢DAB⸣-su u₂[x]-(x)-ru na₄⸢gab⸣-u₂ U₂ ⸢BABBAR?⸣ [...]

(r 5) [...] afflicts [...]: plant of ..., alum, white plant [...]

r 66

[...] na₄ḪAR pu-ṣa-ṣu na₄sisim-kad₂-ru-[u₂ ...]

(r 6) [...] HAR-stone, puṣāṣu-stone, sinkadrû-stone [...]

r 77

[...] x GAZ SIM ZU₂-MEŠ en-še-e-tu₂ ša₂ x [...]; ina še-e-⸢ri?⸣ [...]

(r 7) [...] ... you crush and sieve. The weak teeth of ... [...] in the morning [...].

r 88

[...] bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su 2 GIN₂ U₂ BABBAR ina KAŠ NAG ⸢:⸣ x [...]

(r 8) [...] būšānu-disease afflicts a person [...]: he drinks 2 shekels of white-plant in beer. : ... [...].

r 99

[... u₂]ak-tam ina I₃ [...] ⸢NAG⸣ : * KI.MIN [...]

(r 9) He drinks [...] aktam-plant in oil [...].: (If) ditto: [...].

r 1010

[...] u₂zi-ba-a ina I₃ u KAŠ NAG : * KI.MIN [...]

(r 10) [...] he drinks black cumin in oil and beer. : (If) ditto: [...].

r 1111

[...] na₄gab-u₂ SUD₂ ina GEŠTIN NAG : * KI.MIN [...]

(r 11) [...] you pound alum [...] (and) he drinks (it) in wine. : (If) ditto: [...].

r 1212

EN₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu da-an DAB-su GIM UR.[MAḪ ...]

(r 12) Incantation: "būšānu-disease, mighty is its affliction! Like a lion [...]. Like a wolf it has seized the throat. It has seized [...]. It has raised its throne in the windpipe. Leave [...]. Like the dead man has had to abandon the way (of the living) and the stillborn [...]. You may not return to your seizure!"

r 1313

GIM ⸢UR⸣.BAR.RA iṣ-ṣa-bat nu-ur-za iṣ-⸢ṣa⸣-[bat ...]

r 1414

ina ⸢GI⸣.GID₂.MUR-MEŠ it-ta-di gišGU.ZA-šu₂ ⸢ṣi⸣ [...]

r 1515

GIM UŠ₂ šu-ud-⸢du⸣-u₂ a-lak-ti u₃ ni-id ⸢lib₃⸣-[bi ...]

r 1616

at-ta e ⸢ta-tur⸣ ana ṣib-ti-ka EN₂ <ul> iu-⸢ut-tu⸣-[un ...]

(r 16) The incantation is not mine [...]: (it is) the incantation of Damu and Gula, the incantation of DN [...], the incantation of Gula, mistress of health, Gula [...].

r 1717

EN₂ dDA.MU u dGU.LA ⸢EN₂⸣ dx [...]

r 1818

EN₂ d!GU.LA be-let TI.LA d⸢GU⸣.[LA ...]

r 1919

KA.INIM.MA bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su : DU₃.DU₃-BI u₂ḪAB [...]

(r 19) Incantation: "būšānu-disease afflicts him". : Its ritual: "būšānu-plant [...]".

r 2020

* KI.MIN u₂KUR.RA ina I₃ u ⸢KAŠ⸣ NAG-ma ina-eš : * KI.MIN [...]

(r 20) (If) ditto: he drinks nīnû-plant in oil and beer and he will recover. : (If) ditto: [...].

r 2121

* KI.MIN na₄gab-u₂ ina LAL₃ ḪI.ḪI u₂-na-ṣab : * KI.MIN [...]

(r 21) (If) ditto: you mix alum in honey (and then) he sucks (it). : (If) ditto: [...].

r 2222

EN₂ da-an ša₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su GIM UR.MAḪ SAG.DU ⸢iṣ⸣-[bat ...]

(r 22) Incantation: "Mighty (is) būšānu-disease's affliction! It seized the head like a lion [...]. It seized the mouth. It seized the tongue. It raised its throne among the teeth. [...]. Like the son of the childless did not appear, like the stillborn child did not seize the breast of [...]".

r 2323

iṣ-bat KA iṣ-bat EME ina ⸢bi-rit⸣ ZU₂-MEŠ it-ta-di giš[GU.ZA-šu₂ ...]

r 2424

GIM DUMU LUKUR la i-bu-ra GIM ni-id lib₃-⸢bi⸣ la iṣ-bat ⸢UBUR⸣ [...]

r 2525

KA.INIM.MA bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su : DU₃.DU₃-BI IM.KAL.⸢GUG⸣ [...]

(r 25) Incantation: "būšānu-disease afflicts him." : Its ritual: "kalgukku-paste [...]."

r 2626

EN₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu a-mir GIM UR.MAḪ ana la? ša₂ UR.⸢MAḪ⸣ ša₂ bu-uʾ-[ša₂-ni ...]

(r 26) Incantation: "būšānu-disease is seen like the lion (but) more than that of the lion, (the seizure) of būšānu-disease [...] it seized the uvula. Like a cheetah it seized the jaws. In the soft throats [...].

r 2727

nap-ša₂-ri iṣ-bat ki-i min₃-di-ni iṣ-ṣa-bat la-ḫe-e <ina> ⸢nu-ur-za nar⸣-[ba-ti ...]

r 2828

man!(MI-)<na> lu-uš-pur ana DUMU.MUNUS-ME da-nu ša₂ ANe liš-ša₂-nim-ma tal-li-ši-⸢na⸣ [...]

(r 28) Whom shall I send to the daughters of Anu of heaven (so that) they may bring their vases [...]? (So that) they may draw water from the sea, the extensive sea where [...] defiled woman [...], no impure woman has washed her clothes, no partridge has flapped its wings [...] (and which) no black dog has muddied (and) throw (the water) into his (the patient's) mouth (so that) the fever, the sikkatu-disease, the labaṭu-disease (and) būšānu-disease will be torn off from his mouth!" Incantation.

r 2929

liḫ-ba-nim-<ma> ⸢A⸣-MEŠ A.AB.BA tam-ti₃ DAGALti₃ a-⸢šar⸣ ur-ru-uš-ti [...]

r 3030

u-suk-ka-tu₄ la im-su-u lu-ba-re-e-šu₂ iṣ-ṣur ḫur-ri <<la id-⸢lu⸣-uʾ>> la u₂-[na-as-si-su ...]

r 3131

la id-lu-uʾ UR.GI₇ GE₆ lid-da-a ana pi-šu₂-ma ZIiḫ ša₂ KA-šu₂ um-mu sik-ka-tu₄ la-ba-⸢ṭu⸣  ; u bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu TU₆ EN₂

r 3232

KA.INIM.MA bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DAB-su ana bul-lu-ṭu ša₂ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu DU₃.A.BI ŠIDnu

(r 32) Incantation: "būšānu-disease afflicts him". You recite (it) for the complete healing of būšānu-disease.

r 3333

DU₃.DU₃-BI na₄gab-u₂ u₂ni-na-a SUD₂ ina LAL₃ ḪI.ḪI EN₂ 7-šu₂ ana lib₃-bi ŠIDnu KA-šu₂ tak-ta-na-par ina-eš

(r 33) Its ritual: you pound alum and nīnû-plant. You mix (them) in honey. You recite 7 times the incantation against (it). You repeatedly wipe clean his mouth (and) he will recover.

r 3434

* KI.MIN u₂KUR.RA saḫ-le₂-e na₄ga-bi-i GAZIsar u₂ḪAB gišDAL.DAL.GIŠIMMAR ḪAR pu-ṣa-ṣa ŠIKA gišNU.UR₂.MA

(r 34) (If) ditto: nīnû-plant, cress, alum, mustard, būšānu-plant, date-palm pollen, HAR-stone, puṣāṣu-stone, pomegranate shell and myrrh, gazelle flesh, raisin, māštu-plant, (these) 12 plants you dry up, crush, sieve, mix with parched grains (and then) you apply (it) to his mouth and (gloss: new break) (gloss: application of the mouth (means) bandage of the mouth).

r 3535

u šimSES UZU MAŠ.DA₃ gišGEŠTIN.UD.A u₂MAŠ.TAB.BA 12 U₂-ḪI.A ḪAD₂.A GAZ SIM ina ZI₃.ŠE.SA.A ḪI.ḪI

r 3636

KI KA-šu₂ tu-ṭap-pi-ma ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂ṭe₃-pu ša₂ KA LA₂ KA

r 3737

* NA ZU₂-MEŠ-šu₂ mu-nu u bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂<(GUL)>al ina DAL.BA.AN.NA ZU₂-MEŠ-šu₂

(r 37) (If) the munû-disease or būšānu-disease (gloss: new break) grips a person's teeth (and) blood comes out between his teeth: you pound alum, nīnû-plant (and) black cumin together. You surround your finger with carded wool. You sprinkle lard ... [...]. You dissolve. You rub his teeth (with it) until blood comes out [...] he will live.

r 3838

UŠ₂ E₃-MEŠni na₄gab-u₂ u₂KUR.RA u₂zi-bu-u₂ 1niš SUD₂

r 3939

ŠU.SI-ka mu-ša₂-ṭu NIGINmi I₃.ŠAḪ E SU₃ x [...] ⸢tu⸣-lam

r 4040

ZU₂-šu₂ EN UŠ₂ E₃-MEŠni ta-kar [... TI]uṭ

(catchline)
r 4141

* NA EME-šu₂ eb-ṭe-et-ma [...]

(r 41) "(If) a person's tongue is swollen and [...]". Ninth section of "(If) a person's head suffers from fever". Treatment [...].

(colophon)
r 4242

9u₂ pir-su * NA UGU-šu₂ KUM₂ u₂-kal bul-ṭu [...]

r 4343

DUB md20—MU DUMU ša₂ mna-din DUMU lu₂KID.BAR—d[NIN.URTA ...]

(r 43) Tablet of Šamaš-iddin, son of Nadin, descendant of Šangi-[Ninurta ...].
